all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Lethality DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 189 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +57% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Malady (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Lethality Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 942 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 8% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Physical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 4,272 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Spear Of Desolation: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 29% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Spear Of Desolation: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 28% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Boris deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spear Of Desolation: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 28% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Yin's main threat is Backlash; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Spear Of Desolation: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 29% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Kallari's main threat is Guillotine; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Spear Of Desolation: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 28% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Rampage deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spear Of Desolation: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 28% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Ephemeral Surge” ⇒ poke; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field jungle 47.4% over 22,558 games, magnitude not simulated.
